message:
sdk: expose set_session_name + max_tokens getter/setter on AgentSessionHandle

Closes #62 and #63.

Issue #62: embedders need a public path to rename a live session from
the UI. AgentSession::append_session_info and persist_session were
crate-private, so the only way to update the persisted display name was
to drop the handle and reopen the session file out-of-band.

Issue #63: StreamOptions::max_tokens defaults to None, which makes the
openai-compat provider fall back to DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS = 4096. Tool
calls carrying multi-thousand-line file bodies get truncated mid-call
and the session stalls. Embedders had no way to raise the cap without
forking pi or shipping a wrapper that rebuilds the session.

Add three methods on AgentSessionHandle:

  pub async fn set_session_name(&mut self, name: impl Into<String>) -> Result<()>
  pub const fn max_tokens(&self) -> Option<u32>
  pub const fn set_max_tokens(&mut self, max_tokens: Option<u32>)

The session-name path locks the inner session, calls
append_session_info(Some(name)), and re-persists — same shape as the
existing set_thinking_level path. The max_tokens setters are
in-process-only (not written to session metadata), matching the
thinking-level override semantics requested in #63.

Tests verify:
  - set_session_name appends a SessionInfo entry with the right name
    on the leaf path.
  - max_tokens defaults to None, overrides round-trip, and clearing
    via set_max_tokens(None) restores provider default behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
